## Overview

You are provided with a distributed database setup consisting of 3 shards. Your task is to implement data sharding and query routing across these shards.

## HOST: 10.0.116.184

## Database Shards

- Shard 1: Host, Port 3307
- Shard 2: Host, Port 3308
- Shard 3: Host, Port 3309

## phpMyAdmin (UI Access)

- Shard 1: http://:8080
- Shard 2: http://:8081
- Shard 3: http://:8082

## Credentials (refer to Team Databases):

- Username: your\_team\_name
- Password: password@123
- Database: your\_team\_name

## Connecting via CLI

- Connect to a shard: mysql -h -P 3307 -u &lt;team\_name&gt; -p
- ●
- Connect directly to your database: mysql -h -P 3307 -u &lt;team\_name&gt; -p &lt;team\_name&gt;

## Basic MySQL Commands (for command line)

- SHOW DATABASES;
- USE &lt;your\_team\_name&gt;;
- SHOW TABLES;
- SELECT * FROM &lt;table\_name&gt;;

## Team Databases (Username →database: database Name):

1. Lumos  →  database: Lumos
2.  NULL\_VALUE  →  database: NULL\_VALUE
3. Data\_Squad  →  database: Data\_Squad
4. QueryWorks  →  database: QueryWorks
5. Nexus  →  database: Nexus
6. Interview\_rejected  →  database: Interview\_rejected
7. Codebase  →  database: Codebase
8. Machine\_minds  →  database: Machine\_minds
9. Group\_Safe\_Docs  →  database: Group\_Safe\_Docs
10. ACID\_Alliance  →  database: ACID\_Alliance
11. Dragon  →  database: Dragon
12. Data\_Dynamics  →  database: Data\_Dynamics
13. STML  →  database: STML
14. Infobase  →  database: Infobase
15. SELECT\_\_\_FROM\_IITGN  →  database: SELECT\_\_\_FROM\_IITGN
16. ScaleOps  →  database: ScaleOps
17. Data\_Traffickers  →  database: Data\_Traffickers
18. Almonds  →  database: Almonds
19. Team\_randoms  →  database: Team\_randoms
20. Stava  →  database: Stava
21. Big\_Data  →  database: Big\_Data
22. Scalix  →  database: Scalix
23. Optimiser  →  database: Optimiser
24. SQL\_ed  →  database: SQL\_ed
25. Group\_CIO  →  database: Group\_CIO
26. Datahoarders  →  database: Datahoarders
27. maaps  →  database: maaps
28. The\_Boys  →  database: The\_Boys
29. SELECT\_\_\_Squad  →  database: SELECT\_\_\_Squad
30. Mateen\_Sharief  →  database: Mateen\_Sharief
31. DataForge  →  database: DataForge
32. QueryCraft  →  database: QueryCraft
33. Data\_Hunters  →  database: Data\_Hunters
34. Sanchay  →  database: Sanchay
35. RTX\_Core  →  database: RTX\_Core
36. Chernaugh  →  database: Chernaugh
37. BottleNeck  →  database: BottleNeck
38. Sharkey  →  database: Sharkey
39. P\_and\_team  →  database: P\_and\_team

## Important Rules

- Do not modify other teams' databases
- Do not create new users
- Do not use system databases such as MySQL or sys
- Use only your assigned database
- Use all 3 shards in your implementation

## Your Task

1. Select a shard key
2. Implement a partitioning strategy (range, hash, or directory)
3. Distribute data across shards
4. Modify your application to route inserts to the correct shard
5. Route lookups correctly

A Not secure 10.0.116.184:8081/index.php?route=/

Social 88

M conferences

• important

Self % • To Read • B.tech

6. Handle range queries across shards

phpMyAdmin

Welcome to phpMyAdmin

## Debugging Tip

- To check which shard you are connected to: SELECT @@hostname;

English

Log in @

## Example Workflow

- SHOW DATABASES;
- USE Data\_Squad;

Server:

- CREATE TABLE users (...);
- INSERT INTO users VALUES (...);
- SELECT * FROM users;

## Notes

V

Log in

- phpMyAdmin is only for visualisation and debugging
- Your main implementation should be done via your application
- Ensure no data duplication across shards unless intended
- You will be able to access these shards only from the IITGN network
- Only fill admin and password to log in (keep the server value empty)
